text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Maya Thorne 
Consultant Neurologist  
Centre for Epilepsy  
KCH, Denmark Hill, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Maya Thorne, 

Alex Morgan, DOB: 22-07-1991, Hospital No: H874230 NHS No. 1039485726  
Flat 3, 12 Brook Lane, London, SE15 4AB  

Diagnosis: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures; current pattern reported as ≤ 2 seizures per day, typically brief behavioural arrest with loss of awareness and rare convulsive episodes. Levetiracetam partially effective; Sodium Valproate initiated as adjunct. 

Aetiology: 

Not established despite normal MRI brain and unremarkable metabolic screen to date. 

Other Medical Problems: 

Generalised anxiety symptoms related to workplace noise; seasonal allergic rhinitis managed with OTC antihistamine as required. 

Management Plan: 

- Continue Levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily.  
- Commence Sodium Valproate 250 mg mane for 1 week, then 250 mg mane and 250 mg nocte; plan to titrate to 500 mg twice daily if tolerated; baseline LFTs and FBC done today, repeat in 6 weeks.  
- Buccal Midazolam 10 mg for convulsive seizures lasting >5 minutes; employer to be provided with a copy of the rescue protocol.  
- Arrange outpatient video-EEG telemetry to characterise events and correlate with reported daily patterns; ambulatory noise exposure noted as a potential trigger.  
- Safety counselling provided around childcare setting, water supervision, and working at height; patient reports midday rest breaks are often interrupted, which may contribute to breakthrough events.  
- Fit note provided for adjusted duties and protected rest period; patient will discuss with manager; I can supply supporting documentation as needed.  
- Review in 8 weeks; earlier if frequency exceeds the current pattern of ≤ 2 seizures per day or if adverse effects occur. 

History and Progress: 

This early-years childcare worker describes spells occurring in clusters on particularly noisy days, often during transition times and when multitasking. Most spells are brief (10–30 seconds), with confusion afterwards; a minority generalise with limb stiffening and jerks. Over the last month they report ≤ 2 seizures per day on at least three days per week, interspersed with seizure-free days. They note that midday rest breaks are frequently interrupted by staffing needs, which seems to precede the higher-frequency days. A colleague has captured two events on a smartphone video; features were reviewed in clinic and will be further evaluated with planned telemetry. Sleep deprivation and high ambient noise are perceived triggers. There is no history of perinatal insult, significant head injury, or family history of seizures. 

Medication tolerance: Levetiracetam has reduced intensity but not eliminated events; mood irritability noted initially but improved with time. No rashes. They consented to trial Sodium Valproate after discussion of risks/benefits; contraception in place and teratogenic risks acknowledged with written information supplied. 

Workplace and safety: We discussed practical strategies including pre-planned calm-down periods, noise-dampening strategies (ear defenders when appropriate), and ensuring a colleague is available during outdoor play or water-based activities. A stepwise return to more complex multitasking was recommended as events come under better control. 

Administrative: Employer information pack and rescue plan letter prepared. DVLA guidance discussed; patient currently not driving. I will be happy to speak with occupational health to formalise reasonable adjustments. 

Please contact me if you require further details. I have included slight character misprints in this draft to reflect prior document migration artefacts: Ã‚Â·, Ã©, ï¿½, â€ . 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: current pattern reported as ≤ 2 seizures per day

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the current pattern as '≤ 2 seizures per day' in Diagnosis and reiterated in History and Progress: 'Over the last month they report ≤ 2 seizures per day on at least three days per week, interspersed with seizure-free days.' Rule for multiple frequencies: choose the highest frequency. The maximum stated rate is '≤ 2 seizures per day', which normalizes to '2 per day' as the upper bound of the current pattern.
seizure_frequency_number: current pattern reported as ≤ 2 seizures per day
